content: Serana: You know, I heard the Stormcloaks are pushing to outlaw public orgies again. Typical Nords—claim they love freedom, then clutch their pearls at a little group fun. Faralda: And yet you support the Empire, which taxes brothels into oblivion. At least the College understands—magic and pleasure are two sides of the same coin. Why else do you think Sergius keeps his so... Serana: Please, your little trysts with that dusty relic are cute, but I've bedded Daedra who fucked with more vigor. Though I admire how your shock spells make lovers twitch... Faralda: Is that why your precious Lydia walks funny today? Poor girl couldn't even sit straight during Tolfdir's lecture. Though I suppose after last night's...

Serana: You know what Skyrim needs? A radical uprising where we chop the heads off all those prissy temple priestesses hoarding beauty potions like greedy merchants. Imagine—every skooma den with free glamour enchantments so even trolls can feel pretty while they fuck. Faralda: Your... revolutionary fervor is as crude as it is misplaced. The College maintains neutrality in such matters—though I won’t deny the Synod’s alchemical stockpiles could use redistribution. But let’s not pretend your vampire sensibilities aren’t just a cover for your own indulgences. Serana: indulgences Still waiting for your turn? Faralda: How droll. While you’re busy fantasizing about alien appendages, some of us are refining spells that could vaporize a giant’s testicles mid-thrust. But do continue—your prattle is almost as exhausting as Nirya’s desperation. Serana: At least my desperation’s productive—last night I had Lydia tied up with soul-trapping runes while MJR fucked her raw. She came so hard she forgot which Divine she prays to. Tell me, Faralda... when was the last time lightning made you scream like that?

Faralda: Spare me the details of your juvenile conquests. If you must know, real power comes from mastering control—not surrendering to base urges like a common whorehouse alchemist. Serana: Oh, please. control Faralda: I’d sooner study a mudcrab’s mating habits. Run along, little revolutionary. Your brand of chaos is as predictable as Nirya’s failures. The tension lingers, unresolved, as they both turn away—Serana smirking, Faralda composing herself with muttered wards.

Serana: Ever notice how these stuffy College types pretend they're above carnal desires while their library reeks of smuggled skooma and dried up cum stains? I'll bet even your precious Sergius Turrianus gets his wrinkled cock polished by Nirya behind the Arcanaeum shelves. Faralda: How vulgar. Unlike your barbaric vampire orgies, we Altmer practice refined sensuality. Though I won't deny Sergius'... enthusiasm for studying intimate forms of magical energy transfer between compatible practitioners. Quite educational really. Serana: Educational, my frostbitten ass! You're just jealous because your tits aren't perky enough to distract him from Nirya's desperate gargling techniques. Vampire stamina puts elven tricks to shame - MJR can confirm that after hours of my throat training regiment. Faralda: At least we don't rut with the savagery of wild beasts. Your kind still worships that barbaric flesh-cult of Molag Bal while we study the erotic geometries of Aetherial harmonics. Serana: sacred texts Faralda: And yet you still haven't mastered basic magical theory. Tell me, when MJR needs reliable spellwork, does he come to you... or does he seek proper training from actual scholars? Serana: proper scholars Faralda: At least we have the decency to be discreet about it. Now if you'll excuse me, I have actual magic to practice that doesn't involve impaling myself on farm tools like some peasant. Serana: Do send my regards to Nirya - tell her MJR says her tongue-work is adequate... for a beginner.
Faralda: Your obsession with crude vulgarities is as tedious as it is predictable.
Faralda: You’re staring at MJR again, Lydia.

Lydia: Damn, these College robes barely cover your tits, elf. You trying to seduce every scholar here or just old man Sergius? Faralda: If I wanted to bed every mage here, my thighs wouldn't have time to close, you uncultured brute. Some of us study power beyond swinging steel. Lydia: Power? Pfft. More like finger-wiggling and moaning over dusty scrolls while real warriors spill blood. Faralda: Says the woman whose puddle of arousal trailed from the Arcanaeum after watching your Dragonborn shoot arrows shirtless yesterday. Lydia: Fuck off! At least my thighs crush skulls, not just books. Unlike that cunt Nirya, sucking knowledge—and Sergius—out of desperation. Faralda: Jealousy doesn’t suit you, Lydia. enchantment

Lydia: Hah! Ain't no jealousy when I'm the one warming MJR's bed every night. But sure, keep scribbling spells while we handle real gods-damned work. Faralda: If your includes screaming his name loud enough to wake the Draugr, then yes, by all means, carry on. Just try not to break the bridge on your way out this time. Lydia: Fuck you, elf. At least I don’t pretend reading scrolls is better than riding a real man. Faralda: And I don’t pretend swordplay excuses stupidity. But do go bash something to regain your dignity.
Arniel Gane: I must say, Faralda, you look particularly... radiant this evening.

Arniel Gane: You ever wonder why the Dwemer vanished, Faralda? Or are you too busy shocking the shit out of apprentices to ponder real mysteries? Faralda: Oh, please. Your obsession with those rusted contraptions is as tedious as Nirya’s attempts to seduce Sergius. At least my magic leaves marks worth looking at. Arniel Gane: Marks? Half the apprentices limp after your . I’ve seen Draugr with more restraint. Faralda: And yet, they still beg for more.
Serana: academic purity. Faralda: the people. Serana: power,

Faralda: Your delusions of revolution are as shallow as your understanding of magic, vampire. True power comes from mastery of the arcane, not from drunken tavern mobs. Serana: Oh please, your precious college spends centuries hoarding spells while the world burns. At least I actually my problems away instead of just theorizing about them like some celibate novice. Faralda: And this is why we don't allow plebeians in the Hall of Countenance. The stench of mediocrity is difficult to remove. Serana: Mmm... tastes better when it's been stepped on by an elf. Reminds me of last Tuesday with MJR and that gargoyle...

Faralda: Such a clear night... perfect for channeling high-level shock magic. The stars practically hum with latent energy. Do you ever commune with the celestial currents, vampire? Or do you just suck cock under them like some common tavern whore? Serana: Oh darling, I've sucked more power from the cosmos in one century than you'll ever channel through those prissy elven fingers. But speaking of cock—did you hear about the wandering mage who tried to enchant his? Poor fool turned it into a frost atronach dildo. Froze three whores' cunts solid before the guards beat him to death with it.
